name: Web3 Patterns
title: Web3 and Smart Contract Patterns
description: Essential patterns for Solidity smart contract development, DeFi protocol design, and secure blockchain integration.

axiomAlignment.A1_verifiability: Blockchain state and contract logic are publicly verifiable.
axiomAlignment.A2_user_primacy: Prioritizes user asset security and sovereignty.
axiomAlignment.A3_transparency: On-chain audit trails provide absolute transparency of operations.
axiomAlignment.A4_non_harm: Strict security measures prevent financial loss through exploits.
axiomAlignment.A5_consistency: Follows established EIP standards and secure coding practices.

Proxy-Upgradability.description: Using proxy contracts to allow logic upgrades while maintaining the same contract address.
Proxy-Upgradability.use_when: Building long-lived protocols that may require fixes or enhancements after deployment.
best_practices: Use established libraries like OpenZeppelin Upgrades.
Always separate state storage from logic execution.
Implement multi-sig or DAO governance for upgrade authority.

Pull-Over-Push-Payments.description: Allowing users to withdraw funds instead of sending them automatically in a transaction.
Pull-Over-Push-Payments.use_when: Distributing rewards or refunds to multiple recipients securely.
best_practices: Protect against reentrancy attacks by updating state before the call.
Never loop over an array of addresses to send payments.
Always use 'reentrancyGuard' for functions that handle external calls.
Minimize on-chain storage to reduce gas costs.
Follow the latest EIP standards for token implementations (ERC-20, ERC-721).
Conduct extensive local testing and formal verification for core protocol logic.

anti_patterns.name: Unlimited Allowance
anti_patterns.problem: Requesting users to approve the maximum uint256 as an allowance is a major security risk.
anti_patterns.fix: Request only the necessary allowance for the current transaction or implement EIP-2612 permit.
